name: "MyFi LCD" - WiFi controllable RGB backlight LCD

materials.name: Waveshare LCD1602 RGB Module, 16x2 Characters LCD, RGB Backlight, 3.3V/5V, I2C Bus

description:
The "MyFi" LCD is an LCD which you can control with your phone over your WiFi. You can type any text (limited to 16 characters in every line) into the 2 lines of the LCD.
You also can choose the background light color of the LCD with 3 sliders: red, green and blue. In the app is a color preview over the sliders. The Arduino with the LCD has to be connected to the same network as your phone!
You also need to make sure you have all libraries installed: This project requires Arduino's WiFiNINA library for WiFi connection on this specific board and also the library for the display provided by Waveshare.
You can find instructions on how to install the display library here. To control the LCD with HTTP, you can send a HTTP POST request to http://[ip adress of board]/api/set/txt/[text] to set the first line. For the second line, replace txt with txt2.
For setting the display's color, you can send a request to /api/set/color/[hex]. The hex parameter is just a normal HTML color value without a # in front of it.
I'll upload the code for the android app soon. Have fun!
circuit:
Plug the cable from the LCD into it and connect the cables to the arduino board by using the labels on the display:
LCD Vcc -> Arduino 3.3V
LCD GND - > Arduino GND
LCD SCL -> Arduino SCL
LCD SDA -> Arduino SDA
